title: Syngonanthus chrysanthus
taxonomy: Syngonanthus chrysanthus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. This family is a group of small, herbaceous plants commonly known as pipeworts. The Eriocaulaceae family is part of the order Poales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the Poales order, the Eriocaulaceae family is one of the smaller groups. intro: Syngonanthus chrysanthus, also known as 'Brazilian peppertree', is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Vochysiaceae. Traditionally, Syngonanthus chrysanthus is used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
